retry_reason: Put operation always succeeds; if called when refcount already zero it indicates a use-after-free bug, not a transient failure.
contract_summary: Standard put wrapper for btrfs_ordered_extent: decrements the refcount and, if it becomes zero, performs final cleanup including delayed iput on the inode, freeing csum list entries, and freeing the ordered_extent from its slab cache. After calling, the entry pointer is invalid and must not be dereferenced or passed to any other function. The caller must not call btrfs_put_ordered_extent again on the same entry. This function pairs with btrfs_get_ordered_extent (or retake and initial refcount set).

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement refcount and free the ordered_extent when last reference is dropped; all other operations are necessary cleanup tied to the refcount reaching zero.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test(&entry->refs) operates on entry->refs, which corresponds to the access path entry.refs.refs (refcount_t field refs, then its inner atomic_t refs). The type chain btrfs_ordered_extent--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the structure definition. The operation is a put, consistent with the function name and semantics.
